issues.claim: I have been systematically beaten down by my own smallness; my judgment regarding the correct course of action has evaporated entirely
issues.objection: kārpaṇya-doṣa-upahata-svabhāvaḥ is rendered as 'beaten down by my own smallness' — this captures kārpaṇya (smallness/pettiness) and upahata (stricken) but loses svabhāva (one's nature/disposition). The Sanskrit specifies that it is Arjuna's *very nature* that has been afflicted, not merely his current judgment. Acceptable simplification, not distortion. Similarly dharma-saṁmūḍha-cetāḥ is rendered as 'judgment regarding the correct course of action has evaporated' — this renders cetāḥ (consciousness/mind) as 'judgment' and elides dharma into 'correct course of action,' which is an acceptable secularization for the engineering register.

notes: The pivotal phrase śiṣyas te 'haṁ tvāṁ prapannam (I am Your disciple, surrendered to You) is preserved with full weight: the engineering layer explicitly cites 'śiṣyas te 'haṁ' verbatim in the concrete scenario and names the 'positional rank downgrade' as the operative move. Both śiṣya (disciple/student-stance) and prapanna (surrendered) are preserved doctrinally — the engineering layer correctly names 'apprentice' (a register-faithful render of śiṣya) and treats the request-formulation itself as the structural unlock, which mirrors Ramanuja's reading of prapatti as the founding act and Shankara's reading as upagamana (formal approach). The verse's three explicit components (kārpaṇya, saṁmūḍha-cetāḥ, śiṣyas te 'haṁ) all surface in the engineering layer in correct relation. The brūhi (tell with certainty) imperative is preserved as 'will you teach me'. Falsifiability rules out negotiation-disguised-as-surrender, which exactly mirrors the commentarial requirement that prapatti not be conditional on a preferred outcome. PASS at high confidence.
